title: eriogonum brevicaule subsp. campanulatum
intro: Eriogonum brevicaule subsp. campanulatum, commonly known as dwarf Mormon tea, is a plant species that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an expectorant, and possess antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats. Dwarf Mormon tea can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
